channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bt phone cable, 12th March
item.description:
BT phone line is hanging low In the bushes at no 21 going to no 17 could probably reach the line if tried to touch it

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip, 12th March
item.description:
A child’s buggy and some other bits and pieces has been left near this bus stop. Has been there for 2 days now.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic Island Bollard, 12th March
item.description:
One traffic island bollard has been knocked off its base at this junction. Currently lying on its side.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Washing machine dropped, 12th March
item.description:
Someone just dropped few minutes ago, can you please dispose it?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ledbury Street, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Waste water leaking over pavement, 12th March
item.description:
Grey waste water from plumbing of the flats above Cripsies food outlet on the corner of Liverpool road. The plumbing appears to be from kitchen or bathrooms of the flats above and is leaking out near the flat and food entrance doors. It is going directly across public paved area.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Liverpool Grove, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In front of house 167 Belmont Road, Erith, 12th March
item.description:
Been here for more than four months and is expanding in size and getting deeper as more road surface deteriorates.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Belmont Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Permanent pile of rubbish, 12th March
item.description:
The owner of 18 Beverley Avenue is some kind of building contractor and permanently leaves a big pile of rubbish and other building materials outside the back of his property. As the pile is not on his own property and is on the pathway beside Beverley Wood, it is a clear case of flytipping. Although the composition of the pile changes, it has been present for many years.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22, Beverley Avenue, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed, dangerous vehicle, 12th March
item.description:
What 3 Words - ears.margin.fall Previously listed this report and response was vehicle no longer there. It is, it is illegally parked as not declared as SORN. The DVLA website advises this is local authority issue. It is not in a driveable state and is potentially dangerous as well as illegally parked

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sellindge Close, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Littering incident, 12th March
item.description:
On the evening of 8 March 2026, at approximately 22:48, a dark-coloured SUV (registration EN25 ZVO) can be seen stationary while exiting the car park of The O2 Arena. The driver (or a front-seat occupant) briefly opens the driver’s door and deliberately throws an empty plastic water bottle out of the vehicle onto the road surface. The item lands near the centre of the lane before the traffic begins to move again. This clear act of littering was captured on the following vehicle’s dashcam in the vicinity of The O2 Arena (coordinates approx. N 51.497, E 0.005). Such behaviour contravenes Section 87 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Edmund Halley Way, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water leak, 12th March
item.description:
Water coming up from under the road surface

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leigham Vale, Tulse Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pile of junk, rusty, grim and rotten furniture, 12th March
item.description:
The pile of old furniture is a hazard and a health & safety concern as some of the debris is loose and flying around. Cigarette butts and general dirt, batteries and loose furniture sticking out in the path where children the elderly and general public walk.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Burrage Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deceased fox, 12th March
item.description:
A deceased fox is laying in the alleyway leading to the high street from Ruskin Drive. Someone must have seen it and put a box over the top to hide it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ruskin Drive, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In between 2 Donaldson Road and Brondesbury Road, 12th March
item.description:
Debris on pavement tripping hazard

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Obstruction - Overgrown / overhanging private tree / hedge

item.title: North end Aldershot, 12th March
item.description:
Suitcase and spilled contents now has others dumping on it

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Willesden Lane, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Large overgrown hedge, 12th March
item.description:
This hedge forms the rear garden boundary of 218 Crofton Lane in Lynwood Grove. The pavement width is significantly reduced and would be improved by the hedge being trimmed to the boundary.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lynwood Grove, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped, 12th March
item.description:
Rubbish left by roofing contractors and more household and garden rubbish added to it daily It’s on the green between 91 and 113 Petersham Drive Reported for the third time
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Petersham Drive, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furnitures and other items, 12th March
item.description:
Left at one of the usual spots in Glenister Road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Caletock Way, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole, 12th March
item.description:
There is another massive pothole in the road, between Odette Court (1059 HighbRoad) and David Court which is opposite Odette Court. It’s a danger to traffic (cars and especially two wheeled vehicles)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 995, High Road, Whetstone, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Roadworks there for unacceptable time, 12th March
item.description:
The roadworks and temporary lights restricting traffic to one lane have been on Anerley Road at the corner of Anerley Park for an unacceptable amount of time. Weeks and weeks. If they are not in a position to fix what they have dug it up for they should cover it over and come back when they have what is necessary. They should pay for their problems not the local people. There are traffic jams on this road constantly from roadworks and nobody is ever there doing anything.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Anerley Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Utility Works and Temporary Traffic Lights

item.title: Dumped mattress, 12th March
item.description:
Same people who dumped the rug and the sofa as previously reported. They are using you as you’re free collecting their rubbish!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Plaxtol Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
